title: ranunculus friesianus subsp. strigulosos
intro: Ranunculus friesianus subsp. strigulosos, commonly known as the hairy buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Ranunculaceae family. The hairy buttercup has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its chemical composition, including ranunculin and protoanemonin. The medicinal properties of Ranunculus friesianus subsp. strigulosos are believed to provide relief from various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, making it a potential natural remedy for these conditions. Herbal preparations of the hairy buttercup are typically made from its dried flowers, leaves, or roots, which can be used in infusions, tinctures, or topical applications.
